message:
Twas brillig, 
  and the slithy toves did gyre and gimble in the wabe;
All mimsy were the borogoves,
And the mome raths outgrabe.
"Beware the Jabberwock, my son!
The jaws that bite,
the claws that catch!
Beware the Jubjub bird,
and shun
The frumious Bandersnatch!"

He took his vorpal sword in hand: 
Long time the manxome foe he sought
So rested he by the Tumtum tree,
And stood awhile in thought.
And as in uffish thought he stood,
The Jabberwock, with eyes of flame,
Came whiffling through the tulgey wood,
And burbled as it came!
One, two!
One, two!
And through and through, the vorpal blade went snicker-snack!
He left it dead,
and with its head
He went galumphing back
"And hast thou slain the Jabberwock?
Come to my arms, my beamish boy!
O frabjous day!
Callooh!
Callay!"
He chortled in his joy.
All mimsy were the borogoves,
And the mome raths outgrabe.
Twas brillig,
and the slithy toves
Did gyre and gimble in the wabe;
All mimsy were the borogoves,
And the mome raths outgrabe.
"Jabberwocky"
by Lewis Carroll
The mysterious monster called the Jabberwock...
It is a nameless man who defeats that monster.
Why did that nameless man feel compelled to slay the Jabberwock?
It was a poem written by Lewis Carroll in nonsense verse. The greatest nonsense poem is told as a parody of an epic.
A mysterious monster.
A nameless hero.
Many words that never existed before.
A parody of an epic.
It's just like my own existence...
I walked through the city as the sun began to set.
A city bathed in evening sunlight.
The sky took on shades of orange and yellow, and buildings were dyed a dark purple.
The time when the world sinks into darkness... When the lights come on.
The lights in people's lives come on.

name: Master
message: "Oh, you're early today... Um, you seem like Tomosane, right?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yes, I'm Tomosane. Also, it doesn't really matter, but I'm not early at all. It's already after 6:30."

name: Master
message: "Oh, you really are Tomo-chan."

name: Tomosane
message: "Would you stop that? Call me Tomosane, or Yuuki..."

name: Master
message: "It's getting close to seven. The days really have gotten long. It's still light outside."
Haa... Ignoring me, huh? I even came to see him...

name: Tomosane
message: "It's the second of July, after all. The days will just keep getting shorter from now on."

name: Master
message: "Really?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Days are longest at the end of June. Once you get into July, the days start getting shorter. The summer solstice is in June, you know..."

name: Master
message: "You're right. Still, don't the days feel longer in July and August?"

name: Tomosane
message: "But the days are almost an entire hour shorter in August compared to the summer solstice..."

name: Master
message: "Seriously? It's that much of a difference? Why's that? It feels like the days have to be shorter in June."

name: Tomosane
message: "Well, I don't know the details, but I think it feels like it gets darker earlier in June because the clouds are thicker."

name: Master
message: "The clouds? Oh, so the weather makes it feel like the days are shorter."

name: Tomosane
message: "Probably. During June, this city has clouds covering the sky even in the best of weather, so it never really gets all that bright."
The June sky is always dark.
A person would know that well if they had nothing to do besides look at the sky all day.
You could even start to think there's no such thing as a blue sky in June.
Actually, there's a saying about May skies being the finest of all, and that was in the old calendar. It would actually be June in the modern calendar.
And the cliched May showers are the same.
The phrase "May sky" isn't just referring to good weather.
The phrase actually refers to the rarity of a blue sky in the rainy season of June.
That's how precious a clear sky in June is.
Now that I think of it, I knew a dumb girl who liked the June sky...
The same place as always.
That idiot is always here.
She said it was because this place is closest to the sky.
It's kind of ridiculous to say the roof of an apartment store is the closest place to the sky...
After all, even the apartment building on the other side of the street is bigger.

name: Tomosane
message: "It's raining, you know... Why are you up here on the roof?"
"Huh? The rain's nice. I like it."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's not an excuse. You're gonna catch a cold up here."
"Do you hate the rain?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Yeah, I'm in shock right now, since I just heard someone say they actually liked it. I can't believe anyone would actually want to look at such a dreary sky."
"A dreary sky, huh? I guess you could call it that."

name: Tomosane
message: "The June sky is always dreary."
"Oh, really... I actually like the May sky, though."

name: Tomosane
message: "May, huh... I guess it's sorta poetic if you use the old calendar, but that doesn't change the fact that it's dreary."
"But, you know... the June sky hides that which hangs overhead."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hangs overhead? What are you talking about? These dreary clouds are all that's hanging overhead."
"It's a matter of perspective. To me, it's the ridiculously huge blue sky extending into infinity that seems dreary."

name: Tomosane
message: "A ridiculously huge sky, huh... You're like a poet, but with childish delusions."
"You sound stupid when you throw around the phrase 'childish delusions' all the time like that, Tomosane."

name: Tomosane
message: "Wha―"

name: Tomosane
message: "Y-You're the one who's always saying it to me!"
"Hahahaha, that's not important. Try picturing this. Just imagine how much easier life would be if we could hide away that endless sky lurking above our heads."

name: Tomosane
message: "...I don't see it. The cloudy sky is a lot drearier."

name: Tomosane
message: "Why don't you try picturing this instead? Hasaki could have just washed some clothes and can't put them out to dry."
"June! Dirty clothes smell like Summer Comiket!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh?"
"Sometimes the air conditioner smells like it too... I wonder if they're holding an itty bitty comiket in my air conditioner."

name: Tomosane
message: "What's that comiket thing you're talking about?"
"You haven't heard of it? It's when all the funny looking people with funny hobbies and funny glasses get together to talk to each other in their nasally voices and laugh it up, kinda like Sabbat."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't know about any of that, and what's Sabbat? Well, it must be some kind of manga festival, right?"
"Yeah. Apparently he goes to those kind of events a lot. It's a real pain when he suddenly passes the baton to me out there."

name: Tomosane
message: "Hmm... That's never happened to me."
"You're lucky for that. It's hell. It's cold in the winter, hot in the summer, and it always stinks."

name: Tomosane
message: "Oh, that must've sucked."
"You could at least try to sound interested. Who knows, it could end up happening to you next time."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't know what I would do to all those people if that happened. I'm sure it would never happen to me... I made sure of that."
"Oh wow... So you don't think it's a problem when it happens to me?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Who knows... Not my problem anyway."
This conversation really got off track... Well, that's what always happens when I talk to her.
I unlocked the piano and put my hands on the keys.
The sound of the keys... Did it get tuned? It was done really well.

name: Master
message: "How's the piano? It got tuned this afternoon."

name: Tomosane
message: "Dunno."

name: Master
message: "You should... You're the one who said it sounded nasty because it was out of tune."

name: Tomosane
message: "Then it's fine... It doesn't sound nasty right now."

name: Master
message: "What's that..."

name: Tomosane
message: "It was probably Yuki who said that, not me."

name: Master
message: "Oh, really?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't remember that. Plus, Yuki's normally the one who worries about small stuff like the piano's tuning."

name: Master
message: "Oh... I kind of had a feeling that's what it was. She's always been picky about that kind of stuff."

name: Tomosane
message: "She won't shut up about the piano... Also, Master, did Hasaki come by?"

name: Master
message: "Hmm? She said she would come again tonight, after she went to her mother's place."

name: Tomosane
message: "Her mother? Again? That idiot..."

name: Tomosane
message: "Well, whatever... So she's going to come back tonight. I'll make sure to leave before she comes."

name: Master
message: "No you won't. This is your job. Do it right."

name: Hasaki
message: "Hello."

name: Master
message: "Oh? Welcome back."

name: Master
message: "What? I didn't lie. It's already night out, you know?"
The time was 6:58... The sun hadn't even set. Could you really call this night?
Though complaining to Master wouldn't really help much at this point.
More importantly, Hasaki was in front of me.

name: Hasaki
message: "You were about to leave..."

name: Tomosane
message: "...Yeah, I was."

name: Hasaki
message: "Why would you do that?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Because I knew you were coming."

name: Hasaki
message: "Hmph!"
It was pretty easy to understand her personality... She disappeared into the kitchen with a sullen look.
Mamiya Hasaki...
Mamiya Takuji's little sister...
And of course, since I share the same body with Mamiya Takuji, she's my little sister too. Physically, at least.
But only physically...
I've never even shared the Mamiya name, so she's nothing more than a stranger to me.
So I, Yuuki Tomosane, don't like to get involved with her more than I have to.

name: Master
message: "Don't make Hasaki-chan too mad."

name: Tomosane
message: "How so?"

name: Master
message: "Don't you feel bad for her?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I should feel bad for her?"

name: Master
message: "That's right."

name: Tomosane
message: "Then you could be nice to her so I don't have to."

name: Master
message: "You're still spouting that crap?"

name: Master
message: "You know, if you make her too mad..."

name: Master
message: "Look... She can't focus when she's like that. She can't even do her job when she's like that..."

name: Hasaki
message: "U-Um, Master, I'm sorry... I broke a plate."

name: Master
message: "I heard it. Make sure you don't cut yourself."

name: Tomosane
message: "...I'll leave Hasaki to you. I'm leaving."

name: Master
message: "Huh? You have a job to do."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm going home."

name: Master
message: "You're really leaving now?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Sorry... I got a headache all of a sudden. I probably have the flu."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't want to give it to you guys, and this store does sell food after all."

name: Master
message: "Hehehehe..."

name: Tomosane
message: "Ngh!"
He got behind me in an instant.
Faster than I could believe...

name: Master
message: "Oh, you don't have a fever at all?"

name: Tomosane
message: "I-I'm sure I'll wake up with a really awful fever tomorrow morning..."

name: Master
message: "Then you should be okay till tomorrow morning, don't you think?"

name: Tomosane
message: "N-No, uh..."

name: Master
message: "Besides... You have to work to pay me for that plate."

name: Tomosane
message: "B-But Hasaki broke that!"

name: Master
message: "She broke it because you upset her."

name: Tomosane
message: "U-Um, if you squeeze any harder, I―"

name: Master
message: "Oh, do you think you'd be more compliant if I made you faint?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Wait, a-ah, Master, ngh!"
This fag's skills are beyond those of any normal martial artist... He was an instructor at the jujitsu dojo that Mamiya's grandfather used to run.
He's a fag, so he's not really the kind of person I like to hang around with, but there's no denying his strength.

name: Tomosane
message: "Haa... Haa, haa..."

name: Master
message: "Don't underestimate me. If I tell you to work, then you have to work."
He really is good.
He's completely different from all the other people around here.
He had me the second he got hold of my arms...

name: Master
message: "What are you laughing about? Go get changed."

name: Hasaki
message: "Nii-san."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not your Nii-san... I'm Yuuki right now. Yuuki Tomosane."

name: Hasaki
message: "So Tomosane Nii-san..."

name: Tomosane
message: "You know... My last name is different than yours, so you shouldn't be calling me that."

name: Master
message: "What are you talking about? You can call someone Onii-chan even if you're not related to them."

name: Master
message: "And besides, the two of you are related by blood, Tomo-chan."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's not it... And please don't call me Tomo-chan."

name: Master
message: "Shut up. Why are you worried about junk like that?"

name: Tomosane
message: "It's not junk... That's really unpleasant."

name: Master
message: "Is that why you've got nothing but junk down here?"

name: Master
message: "Look, just a tiny piece of junk."

name: Tomosane
message: "S-Stop... Y-You're going to break it... Stop!"

name: Master
message: "Ahahaha, it's you're fault you're so vulnerable. You should learn the value of an hour of training every day of your life."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not a martial artist."

name: Master
message: "Oh, really? I guess you're just a punk who thinks it's all right to beat up amateurs."

name: Tomosane
message: "Th-That's not..."

name: Master
message: "You act tough, but you're still a child, especially down here. Right, Hasaki-chan?"
Hasaki just...
shook her head, like she was saying...
'Don't ask me, don't ask me.'
She had no idea what to say when Master suddenly asked her a question like that.
I played one of my favorite songs.
...Le poisson reveur.
A composition by the French composer Erik Satie.
Though I just copied it by ear, so it's a little different from the original.
The antithesis of tonal music...
It ignores traditional harmonic progression. The score includes copious examples of unusual counterpoint and chord progression.
This strange music is called furniture music.
In actually playing Satie's music on the piano... there are parts where I can understand what he intended the music to sound like, and other parts where I can't understand at all.
Music is a mysterious phenomenon.
The music my hands are playing was originally a melody in someone's head a long time ago in a distant land.
Whether the composer would admit it or not, that melody was made up through an amalgamation of his thoughts, his emotions, and his life.
And now I'm reproducing one tiny part of that... No doubt, an incomplete fragment.
Was the original melody in his head the same as the melody in my ears now?
How well does the sound of my own playing overlap with the melody Satie first conceived?
A colorful sound.
The melody paints a scene.
Could this melody blend into the background music of his life?
These aimless thoughts ran through my head as my fingers struck the keys.
That's a bad habit... When I get lost in my own head, I can't see the customers.
I'm playing for the customers... I'm being paid to do this.
I have to focus on the music.
But still...

name: Customer A
message: "Kyaa, Tomosane-kun, you're so cool."

name: Customer B
message: "Wow! You're so sexy!"
The old men flirted with me in shrill voices... They weren't listening to the music at all.
They were just making noise.
No... That's what Satie intended this music to be anyway, I suppose.
There's a composition by Satie that's actually titled Furniture Music, after Satie's style of composition.
Apparently the program for the concert where it was first played told people that they needn't listen to the music playing during the interlude.
But of course, when the audience heard the player begin to perform during the interlude, they sat quietly and listened.
Satie told them, 'Please continue talking!'
So maybe... this is exactly how Satie wanted his music to be performed?

name: Customer B
message: "Tomo-kun, you're so cute!"

name: Customer A
message: "Especially between your legs!"

name: Customer B
message: "I wonder if he really is cute down there."

name: Customer A
message: "Of course he is!"

name: Customer A
message: "Let me suck on your cute little guy!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Huh?!"
There's no way Satie wanted this.

name: Customer A
message: "Ahh! That hurt."

name: Customer B
message: "Kyaa! Hit me too! Hit me too, Tomo-kun!"

name: Tomosane
message: "D-Don't crowd me, you fags! How am I supposed to play?!"

name: Master
message: "Come on, don't touch the dancer, don't touch the dancer. And you, don't hit the customers."

name: Tomosane
message: "I'm not a dancer!"

name: Master
message: "Good work. Your next performance is in two hours... Be ready at ten."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't mind playing the piano, but can you get rid of those fags for me? They're really creepy."

name: Master
message: "Oh now, you can't say that about our best customers."

name: Tomosane
message: "I don't care if they're customers, they're nasty. All those fags are nasty."

name: Master
message: "It almost sounds like you're talking about me too when you say that. I'm one of those fags that doesn't care if the person I do it with isn't gay, you know."

name: Tomosane
message: "...Wh-What do you mean by that?"

name: Master
message: "Maybe I should help you out with that cute little guy between your legs..."

name: Master
message: "Oh, you've got goosebumps all over. You're so innocent."

name: Tomosane
message: "It has nothing to do with innocence... It's creepy as hell when a monster like you says that."

name: Master
message: "Oh, really? You're at such a difficult age. Just talking about your little Johnny gets you all embarrassed."

name: Tomosane
message: "No... My age has nothing to do with it."

name: Master
message: "Then what is it?"

name: Tomosane
message: "Because you're a nasty old man."

name: Master
message: "I'm not an old man! That's mean, Tomo-chan!"

name: Tomosane
message: "...Like I care."

name: Tomosane
message: "Look... You're gonna make her break another plate."

name: Master
message: "Oh? I'm sorry... You're a bit young to be hearing about all that, Hasaki-chan. You know, all that talk about two men licking each other's cute little guys."

name: Hasaki
message: "C-Cute little guys?!"

name: Master
message: "Yeah, right here. Let me show you."

name: Tomosane
message: "Knock it off!"

name: Hasaki
message: "Th-That's all right! I-I'm not upset at all! Please, continue with your conversation!"

name: Tomosane
message: "Oh, and... there's no such thing as too young to be hearing about fags blowing each other. That's wrong, no matter how old you are."

name: Master
message: "Oh, really? But it's not all that uncommon at all... you know?"

name: Tomosane
message: "S-Stop! Whoa, stop, get your hands off my fly!"

name: Master
message: "Hehehe... You know I love popping little straight boys' cherries."

name: Hasaki
message: "N-No, no, no!"

name: Hasaki
message: "You can't do that, you can't do that! Not to Tomo Nii-san!"
Hasaki was almost in tears as she stepped in to stop him.
Which is fine, but...

name: Hasaki
message: "No! Go find someone else to do that with!"

name: Master
message: "Huh? I've done that so much I'm about tired of it."

name: Hasaki
message: "Not my problem. Who cares? Go suck your own for all I care!"

name: Master
message: "But my body isn't soft like his is."

name: Hasaki
message: "Who cares?! Anyway, Tomo Nii-san is off limits! You can't lick him or suck him or anything!"
Hasaki was on the verge of tears and rambling about terrible things... Is she all right?

name: Master
message: "But, you know, he has to find someone to give him a good blowjob eventually."

name: Hasaki
message: "Y-You don't need to worry about that! I'm sure we can solve that problem here in the family!"
No, Hasaki... I'm pretty sure you're not supposed to solve that problem in the family.

name: Tomosane
message: "More importantly, Master... It's not like I actually have to find someone to do that."

name: Hasaki
message: "Ah, ah, auuhh..."
Hasaki finally realized what she had been rambling about the entire time. Her face went bright red, and then turned terribly pale.

name: Master
message: "Hasaki-chan, you're like a stop light..."

name: Tomosane
message: "That's your fault, you know..."
This is the crap I put up with every day.
I'm utterly stunned at how stupid it is.
It's not all that bad, though.
I shouldn't be experiencing these days, but... they're not all bad.
But I mustn't forget.
My life here could crumble and disappear at any moment.
My whole lifestyle is precarious... It's built upon a foundation that could collapse without warning.
I might appear normal to an outsider, but my life is completely different from that of other people.
Someday... No, soon... I'll disappear.
That's just how it is.
So I have to be prepared for the moment when I'm cut out of the picture, when I won't be able to experience these days ever again.
Because I can't stay here long... I have to be ready for my own disappearance, no matter when it comes.
"You mustn't covet your possessions, for you may never know what could happen in the future."
Those were the words of a genius who conducted the Leningrad Philharmonic behind the Iron Curtain.
Despite his fame, he lived a very simple life, filled with nothing more than nature, music, and art.
You musn't covet your possessions...
That applies not just to physical possessions, but also those of the heart.
I mustn't have too much in this world.
